relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: The wild goats and deer give birth beyond Job’s supervision.
Their months and birth-times are known without human counting.
They bend, give birth, and release life into the wild.
Their young grow strong and go out into freedom.
The wild donkey lives by a freedom Job did not grant.
Desert and salt land become its home.
It laughs at the city and driver’s shout.
Mountains become its pasture.
The wild ox will not become Job’s servant.
It cannot be yoked to Job’s furrow.
Strength does not mean usefulness to human labor.
Wild power does not guarantee harvest obedience.
The ostrich beats joyful wings with strange wisdom.
She leaves eggs in earth and dust.
They may be crushed or trampled.
Her care looks harsh and fearless.
Not every creature is given the same kind of wisdom.
Yet when she rises, she outruns horse and rider.
The horse’s strength and mane are not Job’s gift.
Its snorting carries terror.
It rejoices in strength and runs toward battle.
Fear does not turn it back.
Weapons rattle around it.
It swallows ground in rage and motion.
It smells battle from afar.
The hawk’s flight is not born from Job’s understanding.
The eagle does not rise by Job’s command.
It nests on rock and stronghold.
Its eyes see food from far away.
Even the fierce cycle of death and feeding belongs to the wild order.
